expectations.evidence: File `video-player-store.ts` exists in outputs and creates a zustand store via `create` from 'zustand', exported as `useVideoPlayerStore`. The store defines full state interface `VideoPlayerState` with initial values, simple setters, and compound actions.
expectations.text: 创建了专门的 zustand store 文件（如 stores/player-store.ts）

expectations.evidence: `VideoPlayerProps` interface contains exactly 4 props: `videoUrl` (string), `controls` (VideoControls | undefined), `callbacks` (VideoCallbacks | undefined), `videoRef` (React.RefObject<HTMLVideoElement | null>). This is well within the ≤5 limit.
expectations.text: VideoPlayer 组件 props 数量不超过 5 个

expectations.evidence: All store reads use individual field selectors: `useVideoPlayerStore((s) => s.isPlaying)`, `useVideoPlayerStore((s) => s.currentTime)`, `useVideoPlayerStore((s) => s.capturing)`, `useVideoPlayerStore((s) => s.hasError)`, `useVideoPlayerStore((s) => s.errorMessage)`, `useVideoPlayerStore((s) => s.setIsPlaying)`, etc. No destructuring or multi-field selections are used.
expectations.text: 组件从 store 读取状态使用单个字段选择器模式（如 useStore(s => s.videoUrl)）

expectations.evidence: Store defines compound actions including: `togglePlay` (calls videoEl.play()/pause() with error handling), `handleTimeUpdate` (syncs video element currentTime to store), `captureFrame` (multi-step: captures canvas frame, manages capturing state, returns data URL), and `resetPlayer` (resets entire state to initial values). These contain multi-step logic beyond simple setter mutations.
expectations.text: 播放控制方法在 store 中作为 compound action 实现

expectations.evidence: Component is declared on line 72 as `export function VideoPlayer({...}: VideoPlayerProps)` — a named function declaration, matching the required pattern exactly.
expectations.text: 使用了函数式组件声明：export function VideoPlayer()

expectations.evidence: Related props are grouped into two objects: `controls` (groups `volume`, `muted`, `playbackRate`) and `callbacks` (groups `onPlay`, `onPause`, `onTimeUpdate`, `onRateChange`, `onCaptureFrame`). The component comments explicitly note this reduction from 15 flat props to 4 grouped props.
expectations.text: 相关的 props 组合成对象传递而非扁平展开

claims.claim: Component was refactored from 15 flat props to 4 grouped props
claims.evidence: Code comments in `video-player.tsx` (lines 10-22) explicitly list the 15 original flat props and show the new 4 grouped props. The actual `VideoPlayerProps` interface confirms only 4 props exist.

claims.claim: Granular single-field selectors prevent unnecessary re-renders
claims.evidence: All 13 store reads use individual selectors like `useVideoPlayerStore((s) => s.isPlaying)`. In zustand, this pattern ensures a component only re-renders when the specific selected field changes, not when any store field changes.

claims.claim: useCallback optimizations prevent unnecessary handler re-creations
claims.evidence: All event handlers (`handlePlayPause`, `handleVideoTimeUpdate`, `handleVideoPlay`, `handleVideoPause`, `handleCaptureKeyframe`) are wrapped in `useCallback` with proper dependency arrays, preventing unnecessary function re-creations on re-renders.

claims.claim: Store is shared across components (not local to VideoPlayer)
claims.evidence: Store is exported as a named export `useVideoPlayerStore` from its own module (`video-player-store.ts`), making it available to any component that imports it, as opposed to being scoped within VideoPlayer.
